scenario.summary: 90-day cash forecast — Osaka capex, at-risk receivables, elevated AP
scenario.situation: Mid-November. The Treasury Analyst needs to assess Meridian's cash position for the next 90 days. Q4 is cash-intensive: $45M in capital expenditure commitments (new Osaka production line), $28M in annual insurance renewals, and year-end bonus payouts ($12M). Meanwhile, two large receivables are at risk (Continental EUR 890K and Pacific Dynamics $415K), and the raw material price spike is pushing AP spend higher than planned. The CFO wants to know: can we fund the Osaka capex without drawing on the credit facility?
dataInputs: Q4 forecast v2 revenue and cost projections
agentSteps: Step 1 — Financial Forecasting provides the 90-day cash flow projection: expected revenue collections based on Q4 forecast v2 ($1.28B), operating expenses, known commitments (capex, insurance, bonuses)

Step 2 — Collections & Receivables provides expected inflows: risk-scored receivables portfolio with predicted payment dates. Flags Continental and Pacific Dynamics as at-risk inflows — $1.3M that may not arrive on schedule

Step 3 — Invoice & AP Processing provides expected outflows: committed AP payments for next 90 days, $18M due next week, $24M the following week, with $8M in discretionary payments that could be delayed

Synthesis: Supervisor consolidates into a unified cash position — base case (ending cash $62M, capex fundable), downside case (cash dips to $14M in week 8 if receivables fail), recommendation to accelerate collections and delay $8M discretionary AP
scenario.expectedOutput: 90-day rolling cash forecast with three scenarios, AR risk exposure, AP optimization recommendations, capex funding analysis, weekly monitoring cadence

downsideCase.trigger: Continental and Pacific Dynamics don't pay, plus 2 more automotive customers delay

recommendations: Accelerate collections on Continental (CFO-level escalation this week)
Delay $8M in discretionary AP payments by 15 days as a precaution
